input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a value for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#BB86FC}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the OS theme at startup **and** listens for changes — no extra wiring needed. When the user toggles system dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically.

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or a handler:

```csharp
// On any BindableObject (Label, Button, Page, etc.)
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };

label.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // Dark

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme<T>(BindableProperty, T lightValue, T darkValue)` is an extension method on `BindableObject` that internally creates the same binding.

### Tips

| Concern | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| **Reuse colors** | Define them in `App.xaml` as `<Color>` resources and reference with `{StaticResource}` inside the `AppThemeBinding`. Or use a full `ResourceDictionary` per theme. |
| **Detect current theme in code** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`. |
| **React to changes** | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged`. |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting. |
